name: Cento Law
description_short: Credit reporting attorneys specializing in FCRA litigation for consumers with credit report errors. Nationwide representation on contingency fee basis—no upfront costs.
description_long:
Cento Law is a credit reporting law firm that represents consumers in Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) disputes and litigation. The firm's attorneys have over 20 years of combined experience handling thousands of credit reporting cases across federal and state courts nationwide. Notably, Cento Law's team previously represented two of the three major national consumer reporting agencies (Trans Union and Equifax), providing them with insider knowledge of how these agencies operate and defend disputes.

The firm primarily offers legal representation for consumers challenging inaccuracies on their credit reports. They pursue money damages for documented harms including loss of credit opportunities, employment loss, emotional distress, reputational damage, and punitive damages where applicable. Cento Law handles both individual cases and class action litigation, and they accept all consumer cases on a contingency fee basis, meaning clients pay no upfront fees and only compensate the firm if they win monetary recovery.

Cento Law distinguishes itself through its dual perspective on the credit reporting industry. Their prior representation of Equifax and Trans Union gives them strategic advantages in understanding how credit agencies investigate disputes and defend inaccuracies. The firm also provides educational resources on their website, including step-by-step guidance on disputing errors, contact information for all three major credit bureaus, and information about common credit report errors based on FTC research showing that 46-70% of credit reports contain mistakes.

This is fundamentally a litigation-based service rather than a credit repair or dispute resolution service. Clients should understand that Cento Law pursues legal remedies through courts rather than working with credit bureaus directly on disputes. The contingency model is beneficial for consumers without legal funds, but it also means the firm is selective about cases they take, focusing on situations with clear legal merit and damages.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Contingency fee basis—no upfront costs to consumers; firm only paid if case wins
20+ years experience handling thousands of credit reporting cases
Prior representation of Equifax and Trans Union provides insider industry knowledge
Nationwide representation across federal and state courts
Pursues multiple damage categories including emotional distress and punitive damages
Provides educational resources with FTC-backed dispute guidance
cons: Litigation-based service requires meeting legal burden of proof; not a quick dispute resolution service
Contingency fee model means firm is selective—not all consumers with errors qualify for representation
Website does not disclose contingency fee percentage or typical settlement ranges
No information about average case resolution timelines or success rates
Limited to FCRA violations; cannot help with other credit issues like debt management or building credit
best_for: Consumers with documented credit report errors who were denied credit, housing, or employment opportunities
Individuals who have suffered measurable financial or emotional harm from inaccurate credit reporting
Consumers whose disputes with credit bureaus were not resolved within the 30-day investigation period
diagnosis: Cento Law is best for consumers with serious, documented credit report errors that caused measurable harm and were not resolved through standard dispute procedures. The primary caveat is that this is a litigation firm, not a dispute resolution service—cases require legal merit and damages, and consumers should expect a longer timeline than traditional credit repair dispute processes.
services: FCRA litigation and representation in federal and state courts
Credit report error investigation and challenge
Damage recovery pursuit (lost credit opportunities, employment loss, emotional distress)
Punitive damages claims against credit reporting agencies
Class action litigation for credit reporting violations
Dispute guidance using FTC sample complaint letters
Mixed or merged credit report resolution
Inaccurate account status correction (incorrectly marked delinquent accounts)
Outdated entry removal from credit reports
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
